title: NZ West Coast Road Trip with Kids — Pancake Rocks, Glaciers, and Rainforest
meta_desc: Explore NZ's West Coast with kids, visit Pancake Rocks, glaciers and rainforest on a family road trip
intro: The West Coast of New Zealand is a thrill for kids, with its unique Pancake Rocks, stunning glaciers, and lush rainforest. However, with long driving distances and variable weather, it's essential to plan carefully. We've done the hard work for you, with a realistic itinerary and must-see stops to make your family road trip unforgettable.

sections.heading: Westland Itinerary
sections.body: Start your journey in Greymouth, the largest town on the West Coast, and explore the nearby Shantytown Heritage Park ($35 per adult, $20 per child). From there, head south to Punakaiki, famous for its Pancake Rocks and Blowholes ($10 per person). Allow at least 2 hours for the scenic drive and rock formations.

sections.heading: Franz Josef and Fox Glacier Reality Check
sections.body: While the glaciers are a highlight of the West Coast, they can be challenging to visit with kids. The Franz Josef Glacier walk is around 1.5 hours return, and costs $35 per person, while the Fox Glacier walk is around 2 hours return, and costs $40 per person. Consider a guided tour or a scenic flight for a more kid-friendly experience (from $200 per person).

sections.heading: Driving Distances and Times
list: Franz Josef to Fox Glacier: 30 minutes

sections.heading: What's Worth It?
list: Pancake Rocks and Blowholes: a must-see natural wonder
Franz Josef Glacier walk: challenging but rewarding for older kids
Fox Glacier scenic flight: a kid-friendly way to experience the glacier
Wanaka: a beautiful town with plenty of outdoor activities for kids

cta.text: Use our NZ Family Travel Budget Calculator

sections.heading: Campervan Rental Options
sections.body: If you're planning to drive the West Coast with kids, consider renting a campervan. We have a range of options available, from budget-friendly to luxury models. Check out our campervan rental guide for more information.
